title: ATANT: An Evaluation Framework for AI Continuity
abstract: ATANT is an evaluation framework for AI continuity that addresses gaps in formal definitions, testable requirements, and benchmarks for continuity systems. It provides a formal definition of continuity as an architectural layer, a model-independent evaluation methodology, a narrative test corpus, compliance levels, and reference implementation results.
contributions: Formal definition of continuity with 7 required properties
Narrative test corpus of 250 stories across 6 life domains
Reference implementation results across 5 test suite iterations

memory_systems.name: MemGPT
memory_systems.description: Introduces an operating system metaphor with tiered memory

memory_systems.name: A-MEM
memory_systems.description: Proposes agentic memory with self-organizing capabilities

architectural_frameworks.name: Continuum Memory Architecture (CMA)
architectural_frameworks.description: Defines 6 behavioral properties for long-horizon LLM agents

architectural_frameworks.name: Narrative Continuity Test
architectural_frameworks.description: Proposes a conceptual framework for evaluating identity persistence

results.limitations: Honest reporting of failures, limitations, and the active frontier

gaps_addressed: A formal definition of what continuity means as a system property
A set of testable requirements that any continuity system must satisfy
A benchmark that measures continuity rather than retrieval accuracy alone
